priority_scoring_path: claude-llm: systematic 15-20% NPS/%Positive discrepancy across all products, 100% reproducible on Android, iOS, and Web, impacts recommendation quality and ranking logic, stale or incorrectly aggregated analytics pipeline
severity_justification: The NPS and %Positive discrepancy is systematic (~15-20%) across all products and all platforms at 100% reproducibility, meaning every SLAP user sees degraded quality signals compared to FK main app. This damages SLAP's core value proposition — recommendation quality and ranking logic are directly affected by stale/incorrect aggregated data. Similar historical bugs FLIPPI-984 (NPS/FK discrepancy, P0) and FLIPPI-1007 (incorrect positive score, P1) confirm this pattern; however, no crash or payment blockage is involved and a workaround exists (users can check FK main app), placing this at P1 rather than P0.
owner_reason: Panyam GouthamSai is the assignee on FLIPPI-984, the highest-similarity historical bug covering NPS discrepancy on the SLAP product page versus the FK page, making them the most likely domain owner for product-page analytics metrics.
